_synthetic_notice: SYNTHETIC — fabricated for demonstration only. No real patient data.

day_2.note: Net negative 2.1 kg after IV furosemide

current.note: Clinically euvolemic. SpO2 back to baseline on room air.

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.flag: INCREASED from home dose. Discharge dose TBD — requires outpatient follow-up to titrate.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.flag: TRIPLE THERAPY RISK: concurrent with furosemide and spironolactone. Monitor K+ and renal function closely.

medications.name: Spironolactone
medications.flag: NEW medication. Hyperkalemia risk compounded with lisinopril. K+ currently 3.8 mEq/L on supplementation.

medications.name: Amoxicillin-clavulanate
medications.flag: Complete 5-day course. Day 1 of 5 started on admission. Verify adherence at discharge.

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.flag: Recheck BMP at discharge or day before. Adjust dose with furosemide titration.

potassium.flag: LOW-NORMAL — monitor with triple diuretic therapy

bun.flag: H — CKD baseline, up from 22 on last outpatient check

creatinine.flag: H — baseline 1.1 (CKD 3a). Mild worsening, likely pre-renal.

wbc.flag: H — trending down from 14.8 on admission (pneumonia improving)

bnp.flag: H — significantly improved from 2,340 on admission

troponin_i.flag: Borderline — stable x2 draws, no ACS

hba1c.flag: Above target — diabetes management to address at follow-up

social.living_situation: Alone. Third-floor apartment. No elevator in building.
social.primary_support: Daughter (Alice Chen), lives 45 min away, works full-time Mon-Fri
social.insurance_type: Medicare Part A + Part B with Medigap supplement
social.health_literacy: Moderate — manages own medications with pill organizer, reads instructions independently

ambulation.current: 15 feet with walker before rest stop required
ambulation.baseline_prior_admission: Ambulated independently within apartment, used cane outdoors

fall_risk.note: Falls x1 at home 6 weeks ago (no injury). Functional decline this admission.

cognitive_screen.interpretation: Mild cognitive impairment — at baseline per daughter

transportation.detail: Daughter can drive 2x/week. No personal vehicle. Would need medical transport or daughter for appointments.

food_security.detail: Receives Meals on Wheels (ongoing). No food insecurity concern.

housing_stability.detail: Third-floor walkup, no elevator. Unable to safely navigate stairs with current functional status. Critical discharge barrier.

medication_affordability.detail: Medigap covers most costs. Spironolactone is new — confirm formulary and copay.

discharge_target.destination_alternative: Short-term SNF if daughter unavailable for stair assessment and teaching
